Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Faster C software with Dynamic Feature Detection 56 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 181 upvotes, 22 comments
  3. Flip Distance of Convex Triangulations and Tree Rotation Is NP-Complete 16 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 124 upvotes, 24 comments
  5. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 278 upvotes, 69 comments
  6. Building a new Flash 315 upvotes, 80 comments
  7. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 213 upvotes, 62 comments
  8. “It turns out” (2010) 241 upvotes, 76 comments
  9. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 112 upvotes, 31 comments
  10. The View from RSS 74 upvotes, 19 comments
  11. Show HN: A shell-native cd-compatible directory jumper using power-law frecency 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  12. An interactive map of Flock Cams 504 upvotes, 190 comments
  13. Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies’ 181 upvotes, 67 comments
  14. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 522 upvotes, 246 comments
  15. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 70 upvotes, 28 comments
  16. A bit of fluid mechanics from scratch not from scratch 30 upvotes, 10 comments
  17. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 64 upvotes, 28 comments
  18. NRC Issues First Commercial Reactor Construction Approval in 10 Years [pdf] 43 upvotes, 18 comments
  19. Googleworkspace/CLI 33 upvotes, 13 comments
  20. Daemon (2006) 13 upvotes, 3 comments
  21. Glaze by Raycast 193 upvotes, 118 comments
  22. Making Firefox's right-click not suck with about:config 265 upvotes, 177 comments
  23. Picking Up a Zillion Pieces of Litter 31 upvotes, 14 comments
  24. Data Has Weight but Only on SSDs 71 upvotes, 47 comments
  25. Was Windows 1.0's lack of overlapping windows a legal or a technical matter? 50 upvotes, 33 comments
  26. Show HN: Vertex.js – A 1kloc SPA Framework 36 upvotes, 23 comments
  27. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 81 upvotes, 68 comments
  28. MacBook Neo 1555 upvotes, 1873 comments
  29. What's Driving Rising Business Costs? 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra 0 upvotes, 0 comments
